title: App crashes on save with encoding error after CSV import containing non-ASCII characters
problem: TaskFlow crashes immediately when the user clicks Save on a task list that was populated via CSV import. A brief error dialog mentioning 'encoding' flashes before the app closes. The crash is deterministic (every save attempt) and began after importing tasks from a CSV file containing special characters such as curly/smart quotes and possibly em dashes or other non-ASCII punctuation.
root_cause_hypothesis: The CSV import path likely ingests text as raw bytes or assumes ASCII/Latin-1 encoding, allowing non-ASCII characters (smart quotes U+2018/U+2019/U+201C/U+201D, em dashes U+2014, etc.) into the task data store. The save/serialization path then fails when it encounters these characters — most likely a UTF-8 encoding or validation step that throws an unhandled exception, crashing the app instead of gracefully handling or sanitizing the input.
reproduction_steps: Create a CSV file with task data containing non-ASCII characters: smart/curly quotes (‘ ’ “ ”), em dashes (—), and other common special punctuation
Open TaskFlow and import the CSV file into a new task list
Verify the tasks appear in the list (they likely display fine)
Observe: app crashes with a brief encoding-related error dialog
impact: User is completely unable to save their task list containing ~200 tasks. All work is at risk of being lost. Any user who imports CSV data with non-ASCII characters will hit this. The crash is deterministic with no workaround available to the reporter.
recommended_fix: 1. Check the save/serialization code path for encoding assumptions — ensure it handles UTF-8 (or whatever encoding the data store uses) correctly for all Unicode characters. 2. Check the CSV import path — it should either normalize special characters on import (e.g., convert smart quotes to straight quotes) or ensure the imported encoding is preserved consistently through to serialization. 3. Wrap the save operation in proper error handling so encoding failures surface a readable error message rather than crashing the app. 4. Consider adding a data recovery mechanism so users can export/backup their task list even when save fails.
proposed_test_case: Import a CSV containing tasks with smart quotes (‘text’, “text”), em dashes (—), accented characters (é, ñ), and emoji. Verify that: (a) import succeeds, (b) save succeeds without error, (c) reloading the file preserves all characters correctly. Additionally, test with CSV files in different encodings (UTF-8, Latin-1, Windows-1252) to ensure the import handles encoding detection gracefully.
information_gaps: Application log file contents (reporter was unsure how to retrieve ~/Library/Logs/TaskFlow/)
Original CSV file is unavailable (deleted after import)
Whether a clean task list (no CSV import) saves successfully — would confirm the issue is import-related rather than a broader save bug
